title: Institutional Research Board
covered_parties: Individuals, corporations, agencies, and institutions, public or private, seeking to conduct a study, survey, or research project on the Coeur d'Alene Reservation.
covered_data: Studies, surveys, and research projects conducted on the Coeur d'Alene Reservation.

claims.claim: An individual, corporation, agency, or institution, whether public or private, wishing to undertake a study, survey, or research project on the Coeur d'Alene Reservation must first obtain a permit approved by the Tribal Council or its designate.
claims.conditions: Does not apply when the study, survey, or research project was specifically requested or contracted for by the Coeur d'Alene Tribe.
claims.cite: Institutional Research Board page, opening paragraph

claims.claim: A person seeking to conduct institutional research on the Coeur d'Alene Tribal Reservation should review the listed documents and submit an application for approval.
claims.conditions: Applies to institutional research on the Coeur d'Alene Tribal Reservation; the page links an external-applicant flowchart, research regulations, and research application.
claims.cite: Institutional Research Board page, application instruction

tsdf_mapping.notes: The page states a research-permit and review process; TSDF tiers do not represent permit issuance, Council approval, or the exception for Tribe-requested or contracted research.

notes: DRAFT machine extraction of the portal page itself, not of the linked research regulations; no Nation-attributed claim is Nation-confirmed.

title: Coeur d'Alene Tribe Research Permit Application
covered_parties: Applicants and researchers conducting research within the boundaries of the Coeur d'Alene Reservation, including participating institutions and project personnel identified in an application.
covered_data: Research activities, human-subject protections, project data and results, reports, publications, and planned public presentations concerning research on the Coeur d'Alene Reservation.

claims.claim: Researchers conducting research in the Coeur d'Alene homeland are expected to act with respect and reciprocity, build community relationships that uphold the Five Pillars, and conduct ethical research that benefits the Coeur d'Alene people.
claims.conditions: Stated as the Tribe's expectation in the application introduction for research conducted within the homeland.

claims.claim: All research conducted within the Coeur d'Alene Reservation boundaries must comply with Tribal laws, ordinances, and codes protecting human subjects.
claims.conditions: The assurance refers to research, development, and related activities supported by project funders or participating Tribal programs or agencies.
claims.cite: p. 3, Assurance of Protection of Rights

claims.claim: By signing the application assurances, the applicant must adhere to the Coeur d'Alene Tribe's research permit regulation ordinance.
claims.conditions: Applies when the applicant signs the assurances in the permit application.

claims.claim: The applicant must attest that information provided in the research-permit application is true and correct.
claims.conditions: Applies to information supplied in the application.

claims.claim: The Tribe may deny or cancel a permit or application when the applicant provides false information.
claims.conditions: Triggered by false information in the application.

claims.claim: The project sponsor or the sponsor's designee may review project data and results 30 days before publication or delivery of a presentation.
claims.conditions: The applicant agrees to this as a special condition of the application assurances.

claims.claim: A copy of every report and publication must be given to the director of each sponsoring department.
claims.conditions: Applies to all reports and publications produced under the permitted project.

claims.claim: Notice of planned public presentations involving the research process or findings must be given to the director of each sponsoring department.
claims.conditions: The application gives conferences and workshops as examples of covered public presentations.

claims.claim: An applicant should attach a project abstract and an outline of the project work plan in addition to answering the project-description questions.
claims.conditions: Stated as a request in the project-description portion of the application.

tsdf_mapping.notes: TSDF can restrict research data and control egress, but it does not encode permit approval, Tribal-law compliance, reciprocity, prepublication review timing, report return, or cancellation remedies.

notes: DRAFT machine extraction of a permit-application template approved as CDA Resolution 036 on 2018-10-25; no completed applicant data were present and no Nation-attributed claim is Nation-confirmed.

title: Nez Perce Tribe Research Regulation Ordinance and Research Permit Sign-Off Regulations/Application
covered_parties: Individuals, corporations, agencies, and institutions, public or private, wishing to undertake a study, survey, or research project on the Nez Perce Tribe, plus applicants and permit holders using the attached process and forms.
covered_data: Studies, surveys, and research projects; information, data, historical and cultural knowledge; research methods, results, products, publications, and participant and Tribal rights.

claims.claim: An applicant must complete the attached forms and present them to the appropriate department manager at least 90 days before the proposed project start date.
claims.conditions: Applies when seeking a written research permit.
claims.cite: Research Regulation Ordinance Process, p. 1, item 1

claims.claim: An applicant must read and obtain a working understanding of the Research Regulation Ordinance from the Cultural Resource Program.
claims.conditions: Applies when seeking a written permit; the Cultural Resource Program has administrative oversight of the permit process.
claims.cite: Research Regulation Ordinance Process, p. 1, item 2

claims.claim: An applicant must prepare multiple copies of a concise one-page prospectus for presentation to the appropriate NPTEC subcommittee.
claims.conditions: The Cultural Resources Director presents the prospectus; the appropriate department or program director may attend.
claims.cite: Research Regulation Ordinance Process, p. 1, item 3

claims.claim: A research permit must be written and must include the authorizing signature and Tribal resolution number to be official.
claims.conditions: Only written permits meeting both stated content conditions are official.
claims.cite: Research Regulation Ordinance Process, p. 1, item 6

claims.claim: A permit fee of $75 must be paid to the Cultural Resource Program upon final approval.
claims.conditions: Payment is due only upon final approval of the request.
claims.cite: Research Regulation Ordinance Process, p. 1, item 7

claims.claim: A copy of the final study, survey, or research project must be provided to the Nez Perce Tribe.
claims.conditions: Applies to the final product of a permitted study, survey, or research project.
claims.cite: Research Regulation Ordinance Process, p. 1, item 8

claims.claim: A person conducting unauthorized research is subject to available civil or criminal remedies under the Nez Perce Tribe Revised Code, including exclusion, criminal trespass, and civil remedies.
claims.conditions: Applies when research was neither specifically requested or contracted by NPTEC nor permitted under the ordinance.
claims.cite: Research Regulation Ordinance Process, p. 1, final paragraph

claims.claim: An applicant must not propose goals, objectives, activities, evaluation tasks, statements, information dissemination, final products, or outcomes that contradict the sovereignty, use-and-occupancy, and reserved-rights assurances stated in the application.
claims.conditions: Applies to the applicant's study, survey, or research project and its dissemination or final products.

claims.claim: The Nez Perce Tribe may withdraw research-permit approval and issue a public corrective statement if a project contradicts the stated sovereignty assurances.
claims.conditions: The Tribe will first notify the responsible applicant in writing and allow 30 days to correct the matter before taking action.

claims.claim: The applicant must protect the rights of individual Tribal members, their families, and the Nez Perce Tribe throughout the project.
claims.conditions: The assurance applies to participants, researchers, and others involved in the study, survey, or research project.

claims.claim: The applicant must use local resources and give Tribal members first preference in project study, survey, and research work.
claims.conditions: Stated as an applicant assurance in the research-permit application.

claims.claim: The applicant must adhere to the Nez Perce Tribe's Research Regulation Ordinance.
claims.conditions: The applicant affirms having read and understood the ordinance.

claims.claim: The applicant must attest that information in the research-permit application is true and correct.
claims.conditions: Applies to information supplied in the application.

claims.claim: The Tribe may deny or cancel a research permit when the application contains false information.
claims.conditions: Triggered by false information in the research-permit application.

claims.claim: A public or private individual, corporation, agency, or institution wishing to undertake a study, survey, or research project on the Nez Perce Tribe must obtain a permit approved through the ordinance process.
claims.conditions: Does not apply to entities specifically requested or contracted for and by the Nez Perce Tribe.

claims.claim: No research permit may be issued without a written agreement with the Nez Perce Tribe; a verbal agreement is not authorized.
claims.conditions: Applies to every study, survey, or research project for which a permit is sought.

claims.claim: The written agreement must address protection of individual and Tribal rights, research methodology, timelines, study results, prepublication review opportunity, final authorization, and publication parameters.
claims.conditions: Required contents of the written agreement supporting permit issuance.

claims.claim: If a product will be published for profit, the written agreement must state the percentage royalty due to the Nez Perce Tribe.
claims.conditions: Applies only when the research product is published for profit.

claims.claim: Before a permit may issue, the applicant must provide, and NPTEC must approve, the applicant identity and signature, purpose and profit status, funding source and amount, methodology, participant names and qualifications, dates and source locations, intended product and publication intent, planned use of results, rights-protection steps, and any performance bond the Tribe deems appropriate.
claims.conditions: The performance bond is required only in circumstances the Nez Perce Tribe deems appropriate.

claims.claim: The Tribe may cancel a conditional research permit if the researcher deviates from the approved study design or the required underlying agreement.
claims.conditions: Cancellation may occur at any time when the stated deviation appears.

claims.claim: The application states that the Nez Perce Tribe retains sovereignty over information, data, and historical and cultural knowledge.
claims.conditions: The same assurance states that written project approval does not mean the Tribe endorses or approves the findings or results.

tsdf_mapping.notes: TSDF can attach restrictive classifications and enforce data egress, but it does not represent the permit, written-agreement, prepublication authorization, royalty, local-preference, Tribal-jurisdiction, or corrective-remedy obligations in this ordinance.

notes: DRAFT machine extraction of the combined sign-off sheet, process, application, and ordinance adopted by NPTEC Admin Action #18 on 2024-10-22; no Nation-attributed claim is Nation-confirmed.

title: Nez Perce Tribe Revised Code Publication Index
covered_parties: Users of the Nez Perce Tribe Revised Code publication index.
covered_data: Code-publication status, amendment notices, and links to the 13 titles of the Nez Perce Tribe Revised Code.

claims.claim: A user of the code publication page must confirm that a given statute is current.
claims.conditions: The page says it is under construction, describes the code as a living document, and states that the Tribe makes every effort to update the posted statutes promptly.
claims.cite: Nez Perce Tribe Revised Code page, introductory publication-status notice

tsdf_mapping.notes: The stored bytes are a code-publication index and currentness notice; they do not state data-classification or sharing controls.

notes: DRAFT machine extraction of the index page itself. Linked enacted titles, emergency amendments, and proposed amendments were not parsed, and no Nation-attributed claim is Nation-confirmed.

title: POL-806 NWIC Institutional Review Board (IRB) Policy
covered_parties: The Northwest Indian College IRB; researchers and institutions whose research is reviewed by that IRB; and participating Tribes, Tribal colleges and universities, and Tribal-based organizations only when they ask the NWIC IRB to review research or otherwise participate under the policy.
covered_data: Research involving or about NWIC faculty, staff, and students, plus research accepted for NWIC IRB review at a Tribe, TCU, or TBO's request; research data, biological and non-biological materials, analyses, presentations, reports, publications, and consent documents.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB must comply with 45 CFR part 46 and NWIC's Tribal-self-determined components and criteria.
claims.conditions: The policy's scope is the NWIC IRB.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB must review all research by or about NWIC faculty, staff, and students and research that Tribes, TCUs, or TBOs ask it to review.
claims.conditions: The second branch applies only when a Tribe, TCU, or TBO asks the NWIC IRB to conduct the review.

claims.claim: The NWIC President must appoint all IRB members, and the IRB must have at least nine members, including at least two federally recognized Tribal descendants with primary nonscientific expertise.
claims.conditions: The policy assigns highest priority to Elders interested in reviewing research.

claims.claim: At least one federally recognized Tribal descendant with primary nonscientific expertise must participate in every full and expedited review.
The NWIC IRB must review all research within the policy's Tribal-self-determined definition.
claims.conditions: The policy's definition includes research involving people not living or identifiable and Tribes, TCUs, or TBOs, and includes some public-health investigations and oral histories.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB must review federally exempt research using its expedited procedure.
claims.conditions: The policy explains that federal exempt categories did not consider potential community harms.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB must require informed consent from participating Tribes, TCUs, and TBOs, not only from individual participants.
claims.conditions: Applies to research reviewed under the policy when those entities participate.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB must review and approve, require modification of, or disapprove all proposed presentations, research reports, and publications.
claims.conditions: The review is to minimize harms and risks, maximize benefits, and recognize self-determination and Tribal sovereignty for participating Tribes, TCUs, and TBOs.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB must give full review to research falling within the federal expedited-review categories.
claims.conditions: The policy states that the federal expedited list did not consider potential community harms.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB must apply criteria under which each participating Tribe, TCU, or TBO owns and controls data, biological and non-biological materials, and analyses about itself and its members and determines access to them.
claims.conditions: The criteria use legally effective terms approved by Tribal-based attorneys and do not apply when the participating Tribe, TCU, or TBO defers.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB may require a researcher's institution to sign a legal Data and Material Sharing and Ownership Agreement with each participating Tribe, TCU, or TBO.
claims.conditions: The agreements contain ownership, control, and access provisions; the requirement does not apply when the participating Tribe, TCU, or TBO defers.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB must report all serious problems and responses to participating Tribes, TCUs, and TBOs and consult their experts before responding to an appeal of an IRB decision.
claims.conditions: Applies to the Tribes, TCUs, and TBOs participating in the research.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB must review cooperative research and must not approve it when NWIC IRB conditions are unmet.
claims.conditions: Nonapproval does not apply when the participating Tribes, TCUs, or TBOs defer.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB must ensure that informed-consent documents contain all Tribal-self-determined benefits, protections, and conditions stated in any Data and Material Sharing and Ownership Agreement.
claims.conditions: Applies when a DMSOA exists.

claims.claim: The NWIC IRB must periodically report its activities to the NWIC President and to every Tribe, TCU, and TBO for which it reviews research.
claims.conditions: Reporting is limited to entities for which the NWIC IRB conducts research review.

tsdf_mapping.notes: TSDF can attach restrictive classifications and control access or egress, but it does not encode collective consent, IRB composition and procedure, entity-specific ownership agreements, publication approval, appeal consultation, or deferral by a participating entity.

notes: DRAFT machine extraction. Northwest Indian College is the issuer. The policy names several Nations and organizations as served by its IRB, but no Nation linkage or adoption is confirmed here; any such applicability requires human and Nation review. The fetched policy also says it will be reviewed every three years, so current-version status should be checked.

title: Kalispel Tribe, Commerce Sign Memorandum of Understanding to Establish Government-to-Government Relations
covered_parties: No party is bound by the stored news release; it describes the Kalispel Tribe of Indians and Washington State Department of Commerce as parties to a separately executed MOU.

requirements.none_reason: The state news release reports an executed MOU and describes intended privacy, security, and information-control themes, but it does not reproduce operative agreement terms.

tsdf_mapping.notes: The release mentions culturally appropriate data privacy and security and Tribe-controlled information, but the MOU's covered data, binding terms, exceptions, and enforcement provisions are absent.

notes: DRAFT catalog of a Washington State Department of Commerce news release, not the agreement text. The release reports that the MOU was signed on 2024-12-05; no claim is attributed as a confirmed Kalispel Tribe requirement.

title: Chapter 43.376 RCW - Government-to-Government Relationship with Indian Tribes
covered_parties: Washington state agencies, statewide elected officials, and the Office of Indian Affairs, as identified only by the stored chapter-index captions.

requirements.none_reason: The stored chapter-index page lists section numbers and captions but contains no operative statutory text from which to extract obligations faithfully.

tsdf_mapping.notes: The index captions concern government-to-government duties, liaisons, training, meetings, and Tribal sovereignty, but the stored bytes do not expose operative requirements or data scope.

notes: DRAFT catalog of the official chapter-index page only. Linked sections were not fetched or parsed, so no statutory obligation is asserted from captions alone.

title: Chapter 70.02 RCW - Medical Records and Health Care Information Access and Disclosure
covered_parties: Patients, health care providers, third-party payors, state and local agencies, and other persons identified by the stored chapter-index captions; any specific Tribal public-health applicability is unresolved from these bytes.
covered_data: Medical records and health care information, including research disclosures, mental-health records, sexually transmitted disease information, and agency-held health information identified by section captions.

requirements.none_reason: The stored chapter-index page supplies titles and captions but no operative statutory text, definitions, exceptions, or conditions for faithful claim extraction.

tsdf_mapping.notes: Health-information confidentiality and disclosure are conceptually related to restrictive TSDF tiers, but the stored index lacks operative requirements and does not establish the advertised Tribal public-health definitions.

notes: DRAFT catalog of the official chapter-index page only. Specific Tribal public-health authority or officer coverage cannot be confirmed without operative section text.

title: WAC 246-455-990 - Data File Fees
covered_parties: The Washington State Department of Health and recipients or requesters of standard, custom, updated, routine-schedule, or department-initiated patient-discharge data files, including Tribes, Tribal organizations within Washington, and designated Tribal epidemiology centers serving Washington Tribes.
covered_data: Standard and custom data files, updates to data requests, routine scheduled data files, and patient-discharge data sharing under chapter 246-455 WAC.

claims.claim: The department must not charge state officials or agencies a data-file fee when they receive data funded through the state general appropriation.
claims.conditions: Applies only to state officials and agencies receiving data funded through the state general appropriation.

claims.claim: The department must collect nonrefundable fees of $110 per standard data file, $50 per standard data file for a student with valid proof of student status, and $110 per custom data file.
claims.conditions: The student rate requires proof of valid student status; subsection (1)'s state-funding exception and subsection (5)'s discretionary waivers remain applicable.

claims.claim: For routinely scheduled data files, the department may enter an agreement with the recipient entity and charge a fee equal to the department's actual costs.
claims.conditions: Applies when data files are provided to an entity on a routine schedule.

claims.claim: The department must assess a fee equal to its actual costs for updates to data requests allowed by WAC 246-455-500(2).
claims.conditions: Limited to updates allowed by WAC 246-455-500(2).

claims.claim: The department may waive data-file fees for local health jurisdictions, for Tribes and Tribal organizations within Washington, for Indian Health Service-designated Tribal epidemiology centers serving Washington Tribes, and for department-initiated patient-discharge data sharing.
claims.conditions: The first three recipient categories concern standard data files; the fourth applies to patient-discharge data sharing initiated by the department.

tsdf_mapping.notes: TSDF tiers do not represent fee schedules, actual-cost agreements, student rates, or discretionary fee waivers for Tribal and public-health recipients.

notes: DRAFT machine extraction of the operative WAC section; the rule permits but does not require the listed Tribal fee waivers.

title: WAC 182-125-0100 - Tribal-Designated Crisis Responder
covered_parties: The Washington State Health Care Authority, federally recognized Indian Tribes, Washington Indian health care providers, Tribal-designated crisis responders, the Washington Indian behavioral health hub, courts, behavioral-health organizations, and local designated-crisis-responder offices.
covered_data: Appointment requests and supporting qualifications, policies and licenses; involuntary-treatment information; Tribal-designated crisis-responder service data; no-bed reports; and crisis-service communications.

claims.claim: The Health Care Authority appoints a Tribal-designated crisis responder after a request and consultation with one or more federally recognized Indian Tribes, or after meeting and conferring with one or more Indian health care providers.
claims.conditions: The rule does not apply to matters heard in Tribal court and does not displace county appointment authority or a crisis-service agency's duty to serve American Indian and Alaska Native individuals.

claims.claim: A Tribal-designated crisis responder must meet the qualifications in RCW 71.05.760.

A Washington Tribe or Indian health care provider may request appointment of a qualified Tribal-designated crisis responder by submitting a recommendation letter with the person's contact and qualification information, training dates, service policies and procedures, and required license, certification, attestation, and exemption documentation.
claims.conditions: The service policies must describe training, backup, information sharing, and communication for responses to private homes or nonpublic settings.

claims.claim: Upon appointment, the authority issues certification and a responder card to the requesting Tribe or Indian health care provider and notifies the listed behavioral-health organizations and courts.
claims.conditions: Triggered by appointment of the Tribal-designated crisis responder.

claims.claim: A Tribal-designated crisis responder serving as an officer of a superior court must submit involuntary-treatment information to the Washington Indian behavioral health hub within 24 hours after an event or as soon as feasible.
claims.conditions: Applies only when the responder serves as an officer of a superior court; timing runs from the response event and allows submission as soon as feasible when 24 hours is not feasible.

claims.claim: The Washington Indian behavioral health hub must submit all responder data concerning service as officers of a superior court to the Health Care Authority within 24 hours of receipt or as soon as feasible.
claims.conditions: Limited to data related to Tribal-designated crisis responders' service as officers of a superior court; timing runs from the hub's receipt.

claims.claim: Tribal-designated crisis responders must comply with no-bed-available reporting requirements under RCW 71.05.750.
claims.conditions: Applies to reporting governed by RCW 71.05.750.

claims.claim: A Tribal-designated crisis responder may request a single-bed certification under RCW 71.05.745.
claims.conditions: The request is governed by RCW 71.05.745.

claims.claim: An authority-designated Tribal crisis responder may operate anywhere in Washington and provide services to any person in the state.
claims.conditions: Applies to responders designated by the Health Care Authority.

claims.claim: A local designated-crisis-responder office should provide technical assistance when requested in a region where a Tribal-designated crisis responder provides services.
claims.conditions: The text says assistance is expected and only when requested.

tsdf_mapping.notes: TSDF can classify sensitive health and service information and constrain egress, but it does not encode the rule's designated submitters and recipients, 24-hour timing, court-officer condition, appointment process, statewide authority, or statutory cross-references.

notes: DRAFT machine extraction of the operative WAC section. Claim categories for mandatory submissions describe governed transfers, not a conclusion that the rule limits all redisclosure.

title: Local Contexts - Grounding Indigenous Rights
covered_parties: Indigenous communities, institutions, repositories, and independent researchers that voluntarily use Local Contexts Labels, Notices, Projects, or Hub accounts.
covered_data: Indigenous cultural heritage, data, collections, genetic resources, samples, research, provenance, contextual metadata, protocols, permissions, access, use, and circulation.

requirements.none_reason: The home page describes voluntary tools, account capabilities, and governance aims but states no binding partner-facing obligation or operative Label, Notice, or Hub agreement term.

tsdf_mapping.notes: TSDF can carry provenance, classification, and access controls, but the home page's community-generated Labels, Notices, cultural protocols, permissions, and cultural-authority semantics are not represented by tier assignment alone.

notes: DRAFT machine extraction of the Local Contexts home page only; linked Label texts, Notices, Hub agreements, and account terms were not parsed.
